phase_name: Community support — issue triage, no active development phase

completed_tasks.name: Issue #91 (woorszt): Explained PKCE vs Keymaster auth difference, asked to test v2.1.8 and try without custom Client ID
Issue #92 (urknall): Explained ~2,700 LOC custom Rust integration layer, go-librespot not feasible
Verified SpotOn works without custom Client ID — local test, logs clean, all flavors use bundled token

decisions.decision: Spotty uses PKCE OAuth (accounts.spotify.com), SpotOn uses Keymaster (hm://keymaster) — different auth flows, Spotty comparison not meaningful for Client ID issues
decisions.rationale: Discovered by reading Herger's Spotty-Plugin source: Settings/Callback.pm uses PKCE_AUTH_URL, API.pm uses _tokenCall with refresh_token grant — no Keymaster involved

decisions.decision: Custom Client ID is unnecessary for SpotOn — bundled token covers all endpoints
decisions.rationale: Verified locally: removed custom Client ID, LMS restart, Browse/Search/Library/Connect all work via bundled token

next_action: Warten auf Rückmeldungen: #91 (woorszt — v2.1.8 test + ohne Custom ID test), #85 (urknall Playlists v2.1.8), #60 (warminskimarcin Cache-Clear), #50 (JesseHoekema RPi3). Backlog: #82 Artist Radio, #55 Context Menu LMS, #32 Library Integration, #7 Search Pagination.
context_notes: Session war reine Issue-Triage. Wichtigste Erkenntnis: Spotty vs SpotOn Auth-Differenz (PKCE vs Keymaster) erklärt warum Custom Client IDs in Spotty funktionieren aber in SpotOn scheitern können. v2.1.8 Fallback + 'einfach ohne ID fahren' sind die beiden Lösungen. Forum ruhig (119 Posts, kein Triage-Bedarf). Upstream: librespot #1724 (IPv6), SqueezeDSP #19 — beide offen.
